{"action":"opened","number":27703,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/pulls/27703","id":2161811036,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAA3dP86A2p5c","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27703","diff_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27703.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27703.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27703","number":27703,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"BUG: correct requirement for acceptable types for stack function call…","user":{"login":"NathanDavisBarrett","id":76917233,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc2OTE3MjMz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/76917233?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett","html_url":"https://github.com/NathanDavisBarrett","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"…s in numpy.core.shape_base.

Currently, an exception will be raised when any object is passed to numpy.stack, numpy.vstack, or numpy.hstack that does not include a __getitem__ method. However, the _arrays_for_stack_dispatcher immediately converts the inputted sequence to a tuple and disregards the initial sequence's type. Here, the tuple constructor requires an __iter__ method, not nececarily a __getitem__ method. Thus, any sequence that has an __iter__ method should be acceptable. Particularly, not all sequences have a __getitem__ method (e.g. queues). But those sequences should still be acceptable here.

{"action":"closed","number":27703,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/pulls/27703","id":2161811036,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAA3dP86A2p5c","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27703","diff_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27703.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27703.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27703","number":27703,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"BUG: correct acceptable types for stack function call","user":{"login":"NathanDavisBarrett","id":76917233,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc2OTE3MjMz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/76917233?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett","html_url":"https://github.com/NathanDavisBarrett","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/NathanDavisBarrett/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"…s in numpy.core.shape_base.

Currently, an exception will be raised when any object is passed to numpy.stack, numpy.vstack, or numpy.hstack that does not include a __getitem__ method. However, the _arrays_for_stack_dispatcher immediately converts the inputted sequence to a tuple and disregards the initial sequence's type. Here, the tuple constructor requires an __iter__ method, not nececarily a __getitem__ method. Thus, any sequence that has an __iter__ method should be acceptable. Particularly, not all sequences have a __getitem__ method (e.g. queues). But those sequences should still be acceptable here.
